title: Mortgages for Medical Professionals
metaTitle: Physician Mortgages in Canada — Resident & Specialist Programs
metaDescription: Mortgage programs for physicians, residents, fellows and dentists across Canada. Student-debt waivers, contract-income recognition and high-LTV physician programs.
intro: Physicians, residents, fellows and dentists have access to lender programs the general public doesn't. The right program can waive student-debt counting, recognize 100% of contract income, and offer up to 95% LTV on a primary residence.

body.heading: Why physician programs exist
paragraphs: Lenders compete hard for medical professionals because they're a low-default group with rising income trajectories. The result is a series of dedicated programs at most major lenders — National Bank, RBC, Scotiabank and TD all have physician-specific desks, as do several credit unions in BC.

Typical features: residency and fellow contracts accepted as qualifying income (rather than waiting for permanent appointments), student-debt minimum payments waived or reduced, 95% LTV available on primary residences, larger insured caps, and lower documentation thresholds for incorporated practitioners.

body.heading: What works best by stage of career
paragraphs: PGY1–PGY5 residents: focus on physician programs that accept the contract-based income, ignore student debt, and offer 95% LTV on a starter home. Fellows: similar story, often with slightly higher caps. Practising physicians: incorporated structure usually allows BFS gross-up plus retained-earnings recognition, opening up Greater Vancouver detached purchase prices. Specialists with multiple T4As: specialty desks that consolidate multiple income streams from hospitals, clinics and locum work.

whoItsFor: Are a medical resident, fellow or practising physician
Have student debt that's affecting your qualifying ratios
Are buying your first home or moving up after residency
requirements: CMA or provincial regulatory body license number
Last 1–2 years of T4s or T4As if available

faqs.question: Are physician mortgage rates better?
faqs.answer: Sometimes — rates may be 5–15 bps below standard pricing at lenders with physician programs. The bigger benefit is usually qualification flexibility, not rate.

faqs.question: Do I qualify as a resident?
faqs.answer: Yes — most physician programs explicitly recognize residency contracts. You'll need a current contract letter and proof of your residency program.

faqs.question: Can dentists access physician programs?
faqs.answer: Many but not all — some lenders extend physician programs to dentists, optometrists and pharmacists. I'll match your file to the lenders whose program covers your profession.
